<findings>

[suggestion] [info] Zone "cli-operations" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [info] export-pdf.ts introduces a binary rendering dependency (PDF) into cli-operations alongside text-output commands; if PDF generation pulls in a heavyweight renderer dependency, extract it to a separate output-renderers zone to prevent that dependency from being loaded for all CLI consumers that never use PDF export
[suggestion] [warning] git-credential-helper.ts handles credential secrets inside the same zone as general CLI output commands; isolate it behind a CredentialHelper interface or move it to a dedicated security-utilities zone so that secret-handling logic has an explicit, auditable boundary and cannot be accidentally imported by non-security callers
[observation] [warning] Bidirectional imports between cli and unit zones (8 + 4 cross-zone imports) indicate a cycle risk; shared types or utilities should be extracted downward.
[observation] [info] High cohesion (0.67) confirms CLI commands are correctly organized around shared output and error infrastructure.
[observation] [warning] prd-epic-resolver.ts references PRD/epic concepts inside the sourcevision CLI zone — verify this doesn't create a hidden runtime dependency on rex.
[pattern] [warning] prd-epic-resolver.ts references PRD/epic domain concepts inside the sourcevision CLI zone; audit whether this file imports from rex at runtime, which would violate the four-tier dependency hierarchy and the gateway pattern without a declared gateway module
[relationship] [critical] cli-operations receives 12 imports from analysis-engine while also sending 5 imports back — bidirectional coupling with a domain layer means cli-operations contains logic that the domain needs, which should instead live in a shared utility or types layer
[anti-pattern] [warning] branch-work-collector.ts lives in cli-operations but collects domain data for branch-work-analysis; moving it to branch-work-analysis would eliminate a cross-layer import forcing analysis-engine to reach into the CLI zone for domain data collection logic
[anti-pattern] [critical] manifest.ts is an analysis pipeline artifact (analysis-engine output) placed inside cli-operations; if analysis-engine imports it, this single misplacement is a direct cause of the critical layer inversion — move manifest.ts into analysis-engine or a shared types layer

</findings>

<insights>

- Good cohesion (0.67) shows CLI commands are tightly grouped around their shared output and error infrastructure
- The prd-epic-resolver.ts inside a sourcevision CLI zone suggests a cross-domain concern that may belong in rex or an integration layer
- cli → unit (8 imports) and unit → cli (4 imports) form a bidirectional dependency that should be broken by extracting shared types to a lower layer
- Zone "cli-operations" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- prd-epic-resolver.ts references PRD/epic concepts inside the sourcevision CLI zone — verify this doesn't create a hidden runtime dependency on rex.
- Bidirectional imports between cli and unit zones (8 + 4 cross-zone imports) indicate a cycle risk; shared types or utilities should be extracted downward.
- High cohesion (0.67) confirms CLI commands are correctly organized around shared output and error infrastructure.
- cli-operations is both a dependent of and a dependency for analysis-engine (5 outgoing + 12 incoming), placing it in a topologically ambiguous position — it cannot be cleanly above or below analysis-engine in any layered hierarchy
- The prd-epic-resolver.ts file in cli-operations introduces a silent PRD domain concept into the sourcevision CLI; if this file imports from rex at runtime it creates an undeclared cross-package dependency that bypasses the gateway pattern
- cli-operations receives 12 imports from analysis-engine while also sending 5 imports back — bidirectional coupling with a domain layer means cli-operations contains logic that the domain needs, which should instead live in a shared utility or types layer
- prd-epic-resolver.ts references PRD/epic domain concepts inside the sourcevision CLI zone; audit whether this file imports from rex at runtime, which would violate the four-tier dependency hierarchy and the gateway pattern without a declared gateway module
- branch-work-collector.ts is classified as [service] inside cli-operations despite its name and likely responsibility aligning with branch-work-analysis domain — misplacement here is a plausible root cause of some of the 12 analysis-engine→cli-operations imports: if analysis-engine needs branch-work collection logic it must reach into cli-operations to get it, creating the layer inversion
- manifest.ts resides in cli-operations yet manifests are analysis pipeline outputs consumed by analysis-engine; if analysis-engine imports manifest.ts this single misplaced file alone may account for multiple of the 12 cross-layer imports driving the critical layer inversion finding
- branch-work-collector.ts lives in cli-operations but collects domain data for branch-work-analysis; moving it to branch-work-analysis would eliminate a cross-layer import forcing analysis-engine to reach into the CLI zone for domain data collection logic
- manifest.ts is an analysis pipeline artifact (analysis-engine output) placed inside cli-operations; if analysis-engine imports it, this single misplacement is a direct cause of the critical layer inversion — move manifest.ts into analysis-engine or a shared types layer
- git-credential-helper.ts is a security-sensitive credential operation co-located with general CLI commands in cli-operations — credential helpers have a distinct threat model (secret exposure, process isolation) and should be isolated behind an abstraction or placed in a dedicated security-utilities zone
- export-pdf.ts introduces a binary output dependency (PDF generation) into cli-operations alongside text-output commands — binary rendering has a different dependency profile and failure mode than text CLI commands, and mixing them inflates the zone's external dependency surface silently
- git-credential-helper.ts handles credential secrets inside the same zone as general CLI output commands; isolate it behind a CredentialHelper interface or move it to a dedicated security-utilities zone so that secret-handling logic has an explicit, auditable boundary and cannot be accidentally imported by non-security callers
- export-pdf.ts introduces a binary rendering dependency (PDF) into cli-operations alongside text-output commands; if PDF generation pulls in a heavyweight renderer dependency, extract it to a separate output-renderers zone to prevent that dependency from being loaded for all CLI consumers that never use PDF export
- [call graph] 362 internal calls, 5 outgoing, 89 incoming (cohesion: 0.99, coupling: 0.01)

</insights>
